frameworks.name: divisive normalization
frameworks.one_line: Surround divisively inhibits center gain to bias perceived orientation.

frameworks.name: subtractive inhibition
frameworks.one_line: Subtractive model fails to capture adaptation-induced orientation biases.

frameworks.name: perceptual filling-in
frameworks.one_line: Center percept replaced by surround via lateral modulation.

core_question: What lateral-inhibition mechanism underlies orientation filling-in in eccentric center-surround stimuli?
key_assumptions: Filling-in reflects lateral modulation of center read-out by surround signals
Selective adaptation isolates contributions of center, surround, and combined channels
Eccentric vision recruits the same mechanisms probed by adaptation
manipulations: Adapter location: center pedestal vs. surround annulus vs. full disk
Adapter vs. test orientation relationship in center-surround sinusoid
key_findings: Surround-only adaptation biased perceived center orientation toward the surround
Center-only adaptation did not produce the orientation bias
Divisive-inhibition model fit biases across all adapter conditions
Subtractive model failed to capture the bias pattern
interpretations: Surround signals laterally bias center read-out via divisive gain control
Filling-in in eccentric vision is mediated by divisive, not subtractive, surround inhibition
